summary: Design and execute a healthcare quality improvement project using PDSA cycles with baseline metrics and sustainability planning.

inputs.name: improvement_scope
inputs.description: The process, unit, or outcome targeted for improvement.

inputs.name: current_metrics
inputs.description: Baseline performance data and current state metrics.

inputs.name: stakeholder_requirements
inputs.description: Requirements from clinical staff, patients, and leadership.

inputs.name: previous_attempts
inputs.description: Documentation of previous improvement attempts and outcomes.

outputs.name: improvement_plan
outputs.description: Detailed improvement plan with aim statement, interventions, and PDSA cycles.

outputs.name: measurement_framework
outputs.description: Process, outcome, and balancing measures with data collection plan.

outputs.name: implementation_roadmap
outputs.description: Phased implementation plan with milestones and accountability.

outputs.name: sustainability_plan
outputs.description: Plan for sustaining improvements and spreading to other areas.

gates.rule: Confirm the task is healthcare quality improvement, not software or manufacturing quality.
Require improvement scope and baseline metrics before planning.
Verify no PHI is present in improvement documentation.
Output must include improvement plan, measurement framework, and sustainability plan.

steps.action: Define SMART aim statement and key driver diagram.
Analyze baseline metrics and establish control charts.
Design evidence-based interventions and change ideas.
Plan PDSA cycles with predictions, data collection, and analysis.
Evaluate patient safety implications of proposed changes.

success_criteria: Aim statement is specific, measurable, and time-bound.
Baseline metrics are established with appropriate statistical methods.
Interventions are based on evidence or best practices.
PDSA cycles include predictions, data collection plans, and analysis.
Sustainability plan addresses spread to other units or sites.

rollback.when: If patient safety concerns are identified, halt implementation and reassess.
